mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in monthly retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side capacity utilization and operational momentum, driving short-term positive equity price adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns during periods of inventory drawdowns or capacity constraints.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates negative real yields and expectations of abrupt future tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets consistently reward negative real yield environments with positive returns, contradicting the tightening expectation mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which systematically supports corporate earnings and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth trends show no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering immediate capital flight from equities and producing negative short-term market returns within five trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: Markets price in recession signals months in advance, resulting in positive 5-day returns following the official GDP confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index signals deteriorating manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, directly reducing corporate profit expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Declining industrial production consistently precedes positive equity returns due to sector rotation into non-cyclical assets masking aggregate market weakness.
